request_title: Change Request — Production Database Migration to PostgreSQL 16
change_description: Migrate the Aurora Logistics primary operational database from PostgreSQL 14.9 to PostgreSQL 16.2. The migration includes upgrading the database engine on all three production nodes (primary + two synchronous replicas), updating the connection pooler (PgBouncer) configuration, and applying optimised query plans that leverage PostgreSQL 16's improved parallel query execution and incremental sort capabilities. The logical replication slots used by the real-time tracking service will be recreated post-migration.
business_justification: PostgreSQL 14 reaches end of community support in November 2026. Migrating proactively allows Aurora Logistics to: (1) maintain vendor security patch coverage; (2) benefit from 20–35% query performance improvements measured during staging benchmarks, directly reducing shipment tracking API response times; (3) enable the data engineering team to adopt SQL/JSON path language features required for the upcoming carrier integration platform scheduled for Q3 2026.
systems_affected:
• **Primary Database Cluster:** pg-prod-primary-01, pg-prod-replica-02, pg-prod-replica-03 (hosted on dedicated bare-metal in Equinix DC5, Frankfurt)
• **Connection Pooler:** PgBouncer instances pgb-prod-01 through pgb-prod-04
• **Application Layer:** Shipment Tracking API (v4.2), Route Optimisation Service (v3.1), Carrier Settlement Engine (v2.8) — all connect via PgBouncer
• **Real-Time Tracking:** Logical replication consumer for the event streaming pipeline (Kafka Connect JDBC source connector)
• **Monitoring:** Datadog PostgreSQL integration, PgHero dashboard, custom Grafana panels
user_impact: During the migration window, the shipment tracking portal will operate in read-only mode for approximately 45 minutes while the primary node is upgraded and the replicas re-synchronise. Internal users (warehouse operations, dispatch coordinators) will see a maintenance banner starting 30 minutes before the window. External API consumers will receive HTTP 503 responses with a Retry-After header during the read-only period. No data loss is expected.
dependencies:
1. PgBouncer 1.22 upgrade must be completed before the database migration (scheduled for March 15, 2026 — CR-2026-0083, approved).
2. Application teams must deploy PostgreSQL 16-compatible database driver versions (already in staging; production deployment gated on this change request).
3. Kafka Connect JDBC source connector must be updated to version 10.7.5 for PostgreSQL 16 logical decoding compatibility.
4. Datadog PostgreSQL integration check must be updated to support pg_stat_io views introduced in PostgreSQL 16.
failure_probability: Low — migration procedure validated three times in staging with production-equivalent data volume (2.8 TB)
failure_impact: High — production database serves all core logistics operations; extended downtime would affect shipment processing for 340+ carrier partners
risk_factors:
1. **Replication slot recreation:** Logical replication slots cannot be preserved across major version upgrades. A delay in slot recreation could cause the Kafka pipeline to miss events, requiring a backfill.
2. **Extension compatibility:** The PostGIS 3.4.1 and pg_partman 5.0 extensions must be verified against PostgreSQL 16. Both have confirmed compatibility in release notes, but have not been tested with Aurora's specific partition scheme.
3. **Connection storm on restoration:** When the primary node comes back online and PgBouncer reconnects, a connection storm could temporarily exhaust the max_connections limit. Mitigation: PgBouncer's server_login_retry configured to stagger reconnections.
implementation_plan:
1. **T-60 min:** Enable maintenance banner on internal portal and send API deprecation notice to external partners.
2. **T-30 min:** Redirect all write traffic to a queue (Redis-backed write buffer) and switch the application layer to read-only mode.
3. **T-0:** Stop PgBouncer instances. Stop PostgreSQL 14 on primary node. Run pg_upgrade with --link mode from 14.9 to 16.2.
4. **T+15 min:** Start PostgreSQL 16 on primary. Run ANALYZE on high-traffic tables (shipments, tracking_events, carrier_settlements).
5. **T+20 min:** Recreate logical replication slots. Verify Kafka Connect offset alignment.
6. **T+25 min:** Upgrade replicas using pg_basebackup from the upgraded primary.
7. **T+40 min:** Restart PgBouncer instances with updated configuration. Drain the Redis write buffer in order.
8. **T+45 min:** Restore full read-write operations. Remove maintenance banner.
9. **T+60 min:** Run automated integration test suite against production endpoints.
10. **T+120 min:** Confirm monitoring dashboards show normal latency and throughput. Close maintenance window.
maintenance_window: Saturday 02:00–04:00 CET (lowest traffic period: average 12 requests/second vs. weekday peak of 850 requests/second)
test_plan:
1. **Staging validation (completed):** Full migration rehearsal on staging cluster with 2.8 TB production-equivalent dataset. All 1,247 integration tests passed. Average query latency improved by 28%.
2. **Pre-migration smoke test:** Verify PostgreSQL 14 backup integrity before starting upgrade. Validate pg_upgrade compatibility check (--check mode) returns no errors.
3. **Post-migration validation:** Run the automated 412-test integration suite covering shipment CRUD, tracking event ingestion, carrier settlement calculations, and reporting queries. Verify Kafka event pipeline delivers events within 5-second latency SLA.
4. **Performance benchmark:** Compare P95 query latency for the top-20 queries against the established staging baseline. Flag any regression greater than 10%.
rollback_plan:
1. Stop PostgreSQL 16 on the primary node.
2. Restore PostgreSQL 14 from the pre-migration physical backup (stored on a dedicated backup volume, estimated restore time: 18 minutes for 2.8 TB via local SSD).
3. Revert PgBouncer configuration to the PostgreSQL 14 connection parameters.
4. Restart PgBouncer and restore application connectivity.
5. Drain the Redis write buffer against the restored PostgreSQL 14 instance.
6. Notify the Kafka Connect team to reset consumer offsets to the pre-migration checkpoint.
7. Estimated total rollback time: 35 minutes.
rollback_criteria:
Rollback will be initiated if any of the following conditions are met:
• pg_upgrade fails or reports data corruption warnings
• More than 5% of integration tests fail post-migration
• P95 query latency exceeds 200ms (2x the current baseline) for more than 10 consecutive minutes
• Logical replication slot recreation fails and cannot be resolved within 15 minutes
• Any data integrity check (row counts, checksum comparison on critical tables) shows discrepancy
communication_plan:
• **T-7 days:** Email notification to all internal stakeholders and external API consumers with maintenance window details and expected impact.
• **T-24 hours:** Reminder notification via Slack (#platform-announcements) and email to carrier partner technical contacts.
• **T-30 min:** Maintenance banner activated on the tracking portal; status page updated to 'Scheduled Maintenance'.
• **T+45 min (if successful):** Status page updated to 'Operational'; confirmation email sent to stakeholders.
• **Rollback scenario:** Status page updated to 'Degraded Performance — Rollback in Progress'; stakeholder notification within 15 minutes of rollback decision.
resource_requirements:
• **Personnel:** 2 Database Engineers (Camille Fontaine, Yuki Tanaka), 1 Platform SRE (Omar Bensalem), 1 Application On-Call Engineer (per rotation schedule)
• **Infrastructure:** Temporary backup volume (4 TB NVMe, provisioned via Equinix Metal API — estimated cost: €280 for 48-hour reservation)
• **Software Licensing:** No additional licensing required; PostgreSQL is open-source
• **Estimated Total Cost:** €1,400 (infrastructure + overtime)
